history: The client, a 28-year-old student, recently returned from Egypt and is managing finances independently for the first time. He previously relied on parental support. He is experiencing a sense of disconnection from past friendships and is concerned about his relationship with Jessica. He is also dealing with job instability and the potential closure of his fraternity house.
intermediate_belief: If I ask for financial help, it means I am failing to be independent. I must handle everything on my own to prove my worth.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't manage my finances independently, I will never be able to succeed in life.
coping_strategies: The client uses philosophical detachment and humor to manage stress. He retreats into abstract thought when overwhelmed and focuses on securing financial stability and supportive relationships.
situation: Considering requesting financial help from parents.
auto_thought: If I ask my parents for help, it means I'm failing. I should be able to handle this on my own.
behavior: He hesitates to ask for help and instead tries to manage on his own, potentially leading to increased stress and isolation.
